ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the customer */,
  "street" TEXT /* Street address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer resides */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the customer resides */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the customer resides */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer */,
  "annual_income" TEXT /* Annual income of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., email, phone, in-store) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details regarding the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel used for the interaction (e.g., phone, email, chat) */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Time taken to respond to the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segmentation criteria */,
  "segment_criteria" TEXT /* Specific criteria used to define the segment (e.g., age range, location) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer providing feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was submitted */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer (1-5 scale) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or additional feedback from the customer */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response to the feedback from the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, marketing, sales) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role assigned to the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer segment targeted by the campaign */,
  "channel" TEXT /* Marketing channel used for the campaign (e.g., email, social media, phone) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaigns_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_marketing" (
  "email_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each email sent */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing campaign associated with the email */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer receiving the email */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date when the email was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the email (e.g., sent, opened, clicked) */,
  "opened" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the email was opened */,
  "clicked" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the email was clicked */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("email_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_marketing_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_marketing_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "phone_calls" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each phone call */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer called */,
  "call_date" TEXT /* Date of the call */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes taken during the call */,
  "call_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the call */,
  "call_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the call (e.g., successful, unsuccessful, follow-up) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_phone_calls_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "in_store_visits" (
  "visit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visit */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer visiting the store */,
  "visit_date" TEXT /* Date of the visit */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details about the visit (e.g., items looked at, purchases made) */,
  "purchase_amount" TEXT /* Total amount spent during the visit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_in_store_visits_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "social_media" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer interacting on social media */,
  "platform" TEXT /* Social media platform used (e.g., Facebook, Twitter, Instagram) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the social media interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of social media interaction (e.g., post, comment, message) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_social_media_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segmentation_history" (
  "segmentation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segmentation entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment the customer was assigned to */,
  "segmentation_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer was assigned to the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segmentation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segmentation_history_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segmentation_history_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance entry */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing campaign */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., open rate, click-through rate, conversion rate) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the performance metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_performance_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)
